system.description: Full-conversion or partial-conversion cyborg built for war, shock force, and survival in the heaviest fighting.
system.notes: Use the Coalition or mercenary combat borg as the default package. The runaway slave-borg variant remains available through the class notes and manual adjustments.

occSkills.name: Language
occSkills.notes: Speak American and one additional language at 96% proficiency.

occSkills.name: Hand to Hand Training
occSkills.notes: Use the full-conversion combat package by default with Hand to Hand: Expert. The slave-borg variant starts with Hand to Hand: Basic.

grantedAbilities.name: Combat Cyborg Conversion
grantedAbilities.notes: Play the default class package as a combat borg with military bionics, reinforced body structure, and battlefield-grade conversion.

grantedAbilities.name: Magic Resistance
grantedAbilities.notes: Apply the class bonus of +3 to save versus magic. Borgs are also immune to bio-manipulation and cannot be read with See Aura.

grantedAbilities.name: Techno-Wizard and Magic Limitations
grantedAbilities.notes: Borgs cannot cast magic, operate techno-wizard devices, or use rune magic as a practitioner. Full conversion destroys psionics completely, while partial conversion halves I.S.P.

grantedAbilities.name: Coalition Borg Standard Capabilities
grantedAbilities.notes: Use the listed military full-conversion baseline for Coalition borgs: P.S. 28, P.P. 20, average Spd 132, built-in language translator, and the standard military conversion package from the bionics section.

grantedAbilities.name: Slave-Borg Variant
grantedAbilities.notes: To play a runaway slave-borg, swap to the peasant-style skill package described in the class text, reduce the starting gear and money accordingly, and use the less specialized bionic loadout.

grantedAbilities.name: Vehicle Restrictions
grantedAbilities.notes: Coalition borgs are usually limited to hover vehicles and motorcycles and are seldom issued robot vehicles or power armor.

unresolvedNotes: The full-conversion military baseline includes fixed physical statistics and bionic hardware that are better handled through actual bionic item packages later rather than faked as permanent attribute bonuses here.
Starting money differs sharply by variant: Coalition enlistment pay, mercenary starting cash, and runaway slave-borg resources remain table-managed.
If the character is only partially converted, keep the class notes for retained psionics and different hardware assumptions in play.
